metadata.name: Senior Capstone Framework
metadata.purpose: Seed the hosted app with the real Senior Project cycle, required submissions, review gates, evidence spaces, deadlines, staff visibility signals, and student guidance patterns from the source PDFs.

sourceDocuments.title: Senior Project Research Proposal Challenge

sourceDocuments.title: Semester 2 Senior Project Mentor Teacher Guide
sourceDocuments.sourcePdf: C:/Users/bryan/Downloads/26 S2 Senior Project MENTOR TEACHER GUIDE.pdf

sourceProcessDiagnosis: The current source workflow depends heavily on students copying documents, moving them into folders, linking final products, emailing staff, and remembering which class gives credit.
The hosted app should replace that fragile link-hunting workflow with first-class submissions, private upload/evidence spaces, clear statuses, review gates, and role dashboards.
The app should still allow external links, but every link must become an evidence artifact with ownership, requirement mapping, review status, and visibility rules.
Students should be guided through each piece in small steps, with quality checks before submission, instead of seeing a long static checklist and guessing what matters.

roles.visibilityGoal: See exactly what is due, what has been approved, what needs revision, and what evidence is missing.
See assigned students, meeting attendance, outlines/readiness, evidence gaps, and comments needed.
See program students, proposal approvals, mentor meeting completion, presentation readiness, celebration evidence, and program-specific blockers.
See cohort-wide completion, program comparison, missing assignments, review backlog, override history, and export/archive health.
See only explicitly permitted dashboards, exports, or support queues.

phaseGroups.title: Setup and Senior Project Folder Replacement
phaseGroups.appTranslation: Replace the copied linked-document folder workflow with a private student workspace that can hold uploads, links, drafts, reflections, and approved artifacts.

phaseGroups.title: Proposal, Approval, and Research Challenge
phaseGroups.appTranslation: Guide students from problem selection through evidence, counterclaim, AI-supported revision, final proposal, and teacher approval.

phaseGroups.title: Mentor Meetings and Presentation Readiness
phaseGroups.appTranslation: Track meeting prep, attendance, missed meetings, outline approval, next actions, and presentation scheduling.

phaseGroups.title: Presentation Day and Celebration Day
phaseGroups.appTranslation: Manage slides, schedule, check-out/check-in, presentation grading, celebration setup evidence, food ingredient requirements, photos, and rubrics.

phaseGroups.title: Reflections, Portfolio, and Personal Archive
phaseGroups.appTranslation: Collect final reflections, best-work evidence, next-year planning, thank-you evidence, export packages, and personal download reminders.

requirements.title: Senior Project Workspace
studentEvidence: Staff can see assigned student workspace
appWorkflow: Create student workspace automatically when the student account is provisioned.
Assign cohort, program, program teacher, and mentor.
Show the student a setup checklist that confirms where uploads, links, drafts, and feedback live.
Do not require students to manage a separate linked-document hub as the primary record.
dashboardSignals: Students with no uploaded or linked evidence
qualityNudges: Your app workspace replaces the old folder/link hunt. Anything important for Senior Project should be uploaded or linked here.

requirements.title: Resume
studentEvidence: Resume upload or private external link
appWorkflow: Provide resume template/help link.
Let student upload a PDF/DOCX or paste a private document link.
Require a quick self-check before marking ready.
qualityNudges: Does your resume show skills, certifications, work-based learning, projects, and program-specific strengths?

requirements.title: Senior Project Proposal Draft

reviewGate.approvalMeaning: The project is feasible enough to keep developing and may move toward final approval.

appWorkflow: Student drafts proposal in the app or uploads a proposal document.
Program teacher reviews for feasibility, pathway fit, safety, scope, and evidence plan.
Student cannot mark final proposal approved until the review gate is approved.
qualityNudges: What problem are you solving?
Who benefits?
What will you actually build, improve, teach, design, serve, or demonstrate?
What evidence will prove the project is more than a simple task?

requirements.title: Senior Project Proposal - Approved
studentEvidence: Final proposal artifact or app-generated proposal

reviewGate.approvalMeaning: The official senior project proposal is approved and can be used by English, Government, and Program classes.

appWorkflow: Copy-forward the approved draft into a final proposal record.
Preserve previous draft versions.
Lock the approved version except through revision/override workflow.
Expose approval status to English, Government, Program, mentor, and admin dashboards according to role permissions.
qualityNudges: A strong proposal should be specific, doable, connected to your program, and clear about the evidence you will produce.

requirements.title: Research Proposal Challenge
requirements.dueLabel: December or January, depending on English teacher

formSections.title: Relevant Evidence
qualityChecks: At least three source records exist.
Each source has title, author or organization when available, URL, and publication date when available.
Key quote is attached to a source and explained in the student's own words.

formSections.title: Counterclaim and Refutation
qualityChecks: Counterclaim represents a real opposing view.
Refutation uses facts rather than only opinion.

formSections.title: Own Words, AI Feedback, Final Version
qualityChecks: Final version improves clarity but still sounds like the student.
AI feedback is treated as revision support, not as the student's replacement voice.
Final version names audience, expected impact, and how the solution works.

formSections.title: Why Finding a Solution Matters
qualityChecks: Student explains who benefits and what improves if the problem is solved.

reviewGate.approvalMeaning: The research support is strong enough to push the project beyond a basic or generic idea.

appWorkflow: Break the assignment into guided sections with save-as-draft.
Show source/evidence completeness before submission.
Warn if the problem, solution, audience, evidence, or counterclaim is vague.
Let staff comment at section level.
Keep AI feedback distinct from final student-authored work.
qualityNudges: Your project gets better when the problem is real, your solution is specific, and your evidence proves both.
Do not stop at 'raise awareness' unless you can show what people will do differently.

requirements.title: Mentor Meeting One Plan of Action
staffActions: Mark attended, missed, or make-up needed.
Submit missed-meeting form or app equivalent for absent mentees.
qualityNudges: Bring your proposal and ask for help on scope, evidence, timeline, and what would make the project stronger.

requirements.title: Mentor Meeting Two Outline

reviewGate.approvalMeaning: The outline is coherent enough for presentation preparation and a presentation time has been scheduled.

staffActions: Approve or request revision on outline.
qualityNudges: Your outline should make the audience understand the problem, your solution, your process, your evidence, and what you learned.

requirements.title: Thanks and Thanks Senior Project Week
appWorkflow: Provide thank-you letter template.
Allow upload/link/paste.
Track completion separately from presentation and celebration readiness.
qualityNudges: Name the specific support you received and explain why it mattered.

requirements.title: Senior Project Presentation Day
staffActions: Track class check-out for scheduled presentations.
Confirm students attend first 10 minutes of each class unless presenting.
qualityNudges: Your slides should support your story, not replace it. Show problem, solution, process, evidence, impact, and learning.

requirements.title: Senior Project Celebration Day
studentEvidence: Ingredient list if food is shared
staffActions: Confirm ingredient list for food items.
Support viewing schedule and student audience expectations.
Use QR/form/paper rubric equivalent as needed.
qualityNudges: Your display should help someone understand what you built, why it mattered, and what evidence proves it worked.

requirements.title: Reflection 1: My Best Work
qualityNudges: Choose work that proves growth, skill, effort, or impact. Explain why it is your best, not just what it is.

requirements.title: Reflection 2: Senior Project Reflection
qualityNudges: Explain what changed from your first idea to the final result, including setbacks and revisions.

requirements.title: Reflection 3: Tenet Mastery Reflection
qualityNudges: Do not only name the tenet. Prove mastery with a specific moment, artifact, or decision.

requirements.title: Reflection 4: Project-Based Learning Reflection
qualityNudges: Show how planning, feedback, iteration, and evidence shaped the work.

requirements.title: Reflection 5: Plan for the Next Year
studentEvidence: Reflection on first year after high school
qualityNudges: Make the plan specific enough that future-you could use it: next steps, supports, dates, money/time realities, and backup options.

requirements.title: Download and Keep Personal Copies
studentEvidence: Student confirms personal download or transfer
appWorkflow: Generate export package of approved documents, artifacts, reflections, and portfolio links.
Warn students that district account access may end after graduation.
Let staff see who has not generated or acknowledged the archive.
dashboardSignals: External links at risk after graduation
qualityNudges: Keep proof of what you built and learned where you can access it after graduation.

crossCuttingAppRules.rule: Any linked Google Doc, slide deck, photo, form response, or external artifact becomes an EvidenceArtifact with owner, requirement, visibility, review status, URL, and last-checked metadata.
Items that require approval must use submitted/under_review/revision_requested/approved status transitions rather than simple checkboxes.
The app should show section-level prompts, examples, and quality checks before students submit, especially for proposal and research work.
Every requirement must map to dashboard signals so mentors, teachers, and admins can see missing evidence, review backlog, attendance issues, scheduling gaps, and revision loops.
Student work, uploads, reflections, attendance, comments, and rubric scores are private records scoped by role and assignment.
The app must support a student-facing archive/export workflow before district account access ends.
